read-tracked-and-untracked-state.label: Read the Community Site State
read-tracked-and-untracked-state.summary: Carry the same community-site files from untracked state into one staged snapshot and inspect the proposal.

sketch1.title: Short Status Is a Compact Map

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.title: Read the Untracked Project Files
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.prompt: The prepared `community-site/` repository has no commits yet. Type `git status` and press Enter. Read the report; do not stage or edit anything.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.hint: Ask Git for the full report and leave the state unchanged.

help.concept: Untracked files exist in the working tree but are not yet part of a proposed or committed snapshot.
help.hint_1: The report should say there are no commits yet.
help.hint_2: The supplied project files should appear as untracked.

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.instructions: The prepared `community-site/` repository has no commits yet. Type `git status` and press Enter. Read the report; do not stage or edit anything.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.title: Stage and Read the Proposed Snapshot
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.prompt: The same five untracked files remain in `community-site/`. Type `git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/old-events.txt dist/legacy-preview.js` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.hint: Stage the five exact paths, then read the compact state map.

help.concept: git add copies selected working-tree versions into the staging area; short status then shows the proposed snapshot in its left column.
help.hint_1: Use only the five paths named in the prompt.
help.hint_2: Every supplied file should appear as a staged addition.

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.solutionCode:
git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/old-events.txt dist/legacy-preview.js
git status --short

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.instructions: The same five untracked files remain in `community-site/`. Type `git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/old-events.txt dist/legacy-preview.js` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you run `git add README.md index.html styles.css notes/old-events.txt dist/legacy-preview.js`?

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.title: Inspect the Staged Snapshot Summary
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.prompt: The same five files remain staged. Type `git diff --staged --stat` and press Enter. Read the file summary; do not commit yet.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.hint: Inspect the proposed snapshot without changing it.

help.concept: git diff --staged --stat summarizes the files already proposed for the next commit.
help.hint_2: The staged state must remain ready for the next topic.

Local events and resources.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A

single-choice-2.title: Understanding git status --short
single-choice-2.prompt: What does the `??` symbol indicate in the output of `git status --short`?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

multi-choice-1.title: Read the Two Columns
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which statements about short status are correct? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

options.a: The left column describes staged changes.
options.b: The right column describes working-tree changes.
options.d: Every listed path has already been committed.

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Unstaged Modification Column
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: In short status, an unstaged tracked-file edit uses the letter `[blank1]` in the right column.
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-1.template: An unstaged tracked-file edit uses `[blank1]` in the right column.

fill-blank-choice-2.title: Untracked File Marker
fill-blank-choice-2.prompt: In short status, a file Git has never tracked begins with `[blank1]`.
fill-blank-choice-2.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-2.template: A file Git has never tracked begins with `[blank1]`.

sketch0.title: Status Compares Three Layers
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: `git status` compares the working tree, staging area, and current commit. Before the first commit, supplied files are untracked. After `git add`, they are proposed for the next snapshot. The first commit is recorded in the following topic, so this milestone stays cumulative.

sketch1.title: Short Status Is a Compact Map
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: `git status --short` uses two columns. The left column describes staged state; the right describes working-tree state. Read the marks as a map of where each path differs, not as decorative output to memorize.

sketch2.title: Inspection Does Not Change State
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: Inspection commands are safe, but `git add` changes the staging area. Read state before and after staging so you can explain exactly what moved and what stayed unchanged.
